choices.content: The Tomb of Philip the Bold

assessment_items.question_content: **Match the artist with the detail from their work.** [[☃ categorizer 1]]
hints: The small fly is from a painting by Petrus Christus, the tools are from a painting by the workshop of Robert Campin, Mary is from a painting by Roger van der Weyden, and the mirror is from a painting by Jan van Eyck.

items: ![Convex mirror framed with small roundels, reflecting two figures in a room.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/21fd63a9d58c265e4fc72ee772945212.jpg)

[[☃ radio 1]]

**What's the title?**

[[☃ radio 2]]

**When was it made?**

hints: The artist is from the workshop of Robert Campin..
The title is the Annunciation Triptych (Merode Altarpiece).

assessment_items.question_content:
**Put the panels of the *Annunciation Triptych (Merode Altarpiece)* in the correct order by dragging.**

[[☃ orderer 1]]
hints: The large annunciation scene goes in the center.
The left panel shows the two donors, and the right panel shows Joseph in his carpenter's workshop.

correct_options: ![Two figures in dark robes, one holding rosary beads, enter through a wooden doorway in a brick courtyard.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/cf356537a39a1924aeb56e5465dfd620.png)

![Painting of the Annunciation showing the angel Gabriel in blue addressing the Virgin Mary in red, seated with an open book inside a detailed domestic interior.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/ab4752efab1e7b91d4def31580b45bf7.png)
![Painting of Saint Joseph in a carpenter’s workshop, seated with tools and wood shavings, with a city view through the window behind him.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/dc66fd8a1920ab54e725862e08babeea.png)

assessment_items.question_content:
**Identify the artist, title and date of this image.**

![Large multi-panel altarpiece with upper panels showing enthroned central figure flanked by saints, angels, Adam, and Eve, and lower panels depicting a lush landscape with groups of figures gathered around the Lamb of God.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/e47b025d16294a866a06822871d16cb3.png)



**Who's the artist?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**What's the title?**

[[☃ radio 2]]

**When was it made?**

[[☃ radio 3]]
hints: The artist is Jan van Eyck.
The title is the Ghent Altarpiece.
The date the altarpiece was completed is 1432.
The *Man in the red turban* is a self-portrait of the artist Jan van Eyck. The monk in a white robe is from a *Portrait of a Carthusian* by Petrus Christus.
The sculpture is a portrait of Philip the Bold (art historians believe), from the *Well of Moses*. The figure with the gray beard is Joseph from the *Adoration of the Kings.*

right: ![Weathered stone sculpture of a man with long hair and a headband, shown in three-quarter view with worn facial features.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/f19689df233c9a2df1693f0e66dc4a79.jpg)
![Portrait of an older man with a white beard wearing a hooded robe, shown in three-quarter view against a stone wall background.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/b3919241deed333685cabcd17dcce92c.png)
![Portrait of a monk with a short beard and tonsure, wearing a white robe, set against a deep red background.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/f8f05b568fc398f420ba0dec374ed013.png)
![Portrait of a man in three-quarter view wearing a large red turban and dark clothing against a dark background.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/3d80211c37fa5db42995739db1377be3.png)
